message:
perf(btree,pager): zero-allocation iterative row counting and :memory: file-size bypass (bd-wwqen.1, B3.4)

Two performance improvements to reduce overhead in hot paths:

1. **count_all_rows_iterative zero-allocation path** (cursor.rs, bd-wwqen.1):
   Rewrites the iterative row counting loop to use the new inline cell pointer
   and child-at-offset readers instead of load_page() + parse. The stack tuple
   now carries header_size to enable read_cell_pointer_inline() on parent
   re-traversal. Page data is read directly via pager.read_page_data() and
   headers parsed with parse_page_header(), bypassing the full CacheEntry
   loading path. This eliminates the per-page Vec<u16> cell_pointers allocation
   and the parse_cell_at overhead for interior pages, making COUNT(*) tree
   scans allocation-free in the hot loop.

2. **:memory: committed_db_file_size_bytes fast path** (pager.rs, B3.4):
   After retained-commit on in-memory databases, the committed file size is
   now computed arithmetically (db_size * page_size) instead of calling
   db_file.file_size() through the VFS layer. Since MemoryFile has no actual
   filesystem backing, the VFS roundtrip was pure overhead.

Also updates beads issue tracker.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
